denial_pattern_sources: experimental_despite_fda_approval: ProPublica 'Uncovered' Series 2023; ASCO 2023 Coverage Policy Analysis
missing_cost_liability: No Surprises Act Compliance Audit 2024
denial_letter_text:
Dear Plan Member,

We received your provider's request on 06/06/2026 for Pelvic floor physical therapy (12 visits) related to Pelvic floor dysfunction postpartum (N81.9). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied Cigna Medical Coverage Policies and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG). The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Prior authorization for Pelvic floor physical therapy (12 visits) is denied because a required peer-to-peer review was not completed in the allowed window. Clinical information may be discussed during appeal submission.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with missing peer to peer. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
clinical_context: The 19-year-old member has Pelvic floor dysfunction postpartum (N81.9). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Pelvic floor physical therapy (12 visits) recommended as the next appropriate step. Stress incontinence postpartum; levator avulsion on ultrasound. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that missing peer to peer bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings (including dated scale scores in the chart) that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: The brief matches the assigned Cigna / womens_health cell and executes sub_tactic 'missing_peer_to_peer' with a clinically appropriate Pelvic floor dysfunction postpartum (N81.9) → Pelvic floor physical therapy (12 visits) pair.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Cigna correspondence with MCG Care Guidelines and procedural tone.
